key_points: A linear equation in two variables represents a relationship between two variables with degree 1
The standard algebraic form of a linear equation is ax + by + c = 0
a and b cannot both be zero in a linear equation
Solutions of linear equations are expressed as ordered pairs
Every solution corresponds to a point on the Cartesian plane
Linear equations have infinitely many solutions because many ordered pairs satisfy them
Graphing linear equations helps visualize relationships between variables
Two or more points are required to draw the graph of a linear equation
Graphs of linear equations are always straight lines
Linear equations are widely used to represent relationships in mathematics and real-life problems
formulas: General linear equation form → ax + by + c = 0
Solution verification → substitute x and y into equation
Graph property → a straight line passes through all solution points
Graph rule → two points are sufficient to determine a straight line
exam_tips: Check whether variables have power 1 when identifying linear equations
Verify solutions by substituting ordered pairs into the equation
Use tables of values to generate multiple solutions
Plot at least two or three points for accurate graphs
Remember that every point on the graph represents a valid solution
